label: Operational Relevance
description: Model for how strongly an evidence statement should influence operational planning, resource design and workflow execution.
usageNotes: Operational relevance is complementary to clinical strength, not a replacement for it.
Use this model to prioritize pathway engineering, resource allocation and readiness gates.

entries.label: Low
entries.description: Limited operational implications beyond routine care.
usageNotes: Minimal effect on staffing, workflow or capital resources.

entries.label: Moderate
entries.description: Meaningful operational implications requiring local planning.
usageNotes: May alter workflow, scheduling or moderate resource consumption.

entries.label: High
entries.description: Strong operational implications affecting resources, coordination or throughput.
usageNotes: Should be visible in route design and pathway governance.

entries.label: Transformational
entries.description: Materially changes care delivery model, capacity planning or institutional capability.
usageNotes: Requires executive, service-line or governance board attention.
